title: San Blas Boat Rental: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Setting off from Cartagena for a day on the water in San Blas with ‘yacht charter in San Blas’ through BoatHire24 offers a taste of paradise from around €150 per person for a shared tour. These pristine islands and anchorages are a Caribbean dream reached by motor or sail.

Where to set off in San Blas

Most charters depart from Cartagena’s yacht harbour, passing through the Panama Canal approaches. Popular anchorages include the Holandés Cays, the lemon-coloured sands of Coco Bandero, and the traditional Guna community of Isla Perro. Other frequent stops are the anchorage near Waisalatupu and the calm waters of the East Lemmon Cays. The entire archipelago comprises hundreds of islands scattered over 100 square nautical miles. You can yacht charter in San Blas for your chosen dates.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in San Blas?

Expect to pay between €150 and €450 per person for a full-day crewed charter depending on group size and boat type. This typically includes lunch, snorkel gear, and a local guide. Prices rise during the high season from December to April when the trade winds keep the sea calm.

  • Shared group tour: €150, €200 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day: €500, €700 for up to 6 guests
  • Catamaran full-day: €900, €1,400 for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed liveaboard yacht: €250, €400 per person per day including meals

Which boat to rent

  1. Power catamaran, best for stability and space; ideal for families, sleeps 8, 12 from €900 full-day.
  2. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon), quiet and eco-friendly; good for cruising, up to 10 guests from €700 full-day.
  3. Motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker), fast and luxurious; perfect for a romantic day, up to 6 guests from €1,200 half-day.
  4. Speedboat (e.g. Axopar), nimble for exploring small coves; carries 6 guests from €500 half-day.
  5. Guna-style panga, local wooden boat; authentic but basic, up to 4 guests from €200 half-day.

For a well-reviewed option, browse the Salty Sun Charters charter page which offers a 12-metre motor yacht with a local skipper and lunch included.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in San Blas?

For bareboat rental, you will need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent; for fully crewed charter, no licence is required. The operator will confirm the exact requirements for each boat. Most visitors opt for a skippered charter to enjoy the scenery without worry.

Tip: book a crewed catamaran with an early morning departure to reach the swimming spots before the crowd, especially during the calm April trade winds.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150, €1,400 per trip
  • Peak months: December, April
  • Typical trip length: 6, 8 hours or multi-day
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR (Euro)

Questions about boat rental in San Blas

What is the best time to visit San Blas for boating?

The dry season from December to April offers steady trade winds and calm seas, ideal for island hopping.

Can we stay overnight on the boat in San Blas?

Yes, many catamarans and yachts offer overnight stays, often anchored near Isla Perro or in the Holandés Cays.

What meals are included on a day charter?

Most full-day charters include a fresh seafood lunch, fruit, and drinks; check the listing for details.

How far is San Blas from Cartagena?

The archipelago lies approximately 30 nautical miles northeast of Cartagena, a 45, 60 minute boat ride.

Whether you choose a shared tour or a private catamaran, exploring San Blas by boat is an unforgettable Caribbean experience.
